response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said's career is centered almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and frontend technologies, with only incidental Java exposure at Altom Consulting in 2016–2018. There is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM-depth work. The closest relevant experience is writing C/C++ components for a C IDE at National Instruments (2014–2015), which is both stack-mismatched and over a decade old. The role demands deep JVM expertise and enterprise framework ownership — areas where this candidate has no demonstrable track record.
strengths: Prior exposure to IDE internals via NI LabWindows/CVI, showing at least some historical interest in developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) that align with quality expectations for a large-scale codebase
Broad polyglot background across many languages and domains, suggesting adaptability
concerns: Primary technical expertise is TypeScript/Node.js with no meaningful Java or Kotlin production experience in recent years
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework experience
The only IDE tooling work (NI, 2014–2015) is C-based, over a decade old, and lasted only six months

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE tooling exposure at NI
